raw.population: N: 121 (8 American Indian; 30 Black; 4 Hispanic; 83 White; 1 Other) Age: 70.5 (60-96)
raw.objective: “to determine the relevant population pharmacokinetic parameters of carbamazepine in a population of community-dwelling elderly subjects and to evaluate the impact of eighteen commonly available covariates on drug dosing requirements through an effect on clearance.”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, BMI, body surface area, ideal body weight, lean body weight, race (White/Non-white), age, study center, PHT, alcohol use, smoking, ALT, albumin, total protein, blood urea nitrogen, serum creatinine, CLcr (Cockroft-Gault) Included: PHT on CL
raw.observations: Out of 18 variables only 1 significant covariate. Weight not a factor (probably due to only elderly subjects). Ka fixed to literature value. Phenytoin comedication increases CBZ clearance in elderly by 23%, dosing should be adjusted accordingly. As can be seen from observed vs predicted concentrations, much variability remains unexplained
raw.equations: Ka = theta1 Vd/F = theta2*exp(eta1) CL/F = theta3*(1+theta4*PHT)*exp(eta2) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes=1,no=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.197 theta2=102 theta3=3.59 theta4=1.23 omega1=0.424 omega2=0.864 sigma1=0.501

extraction_target.title: Population pharmacokinetics of carbamazepine in elderly patients.
extraction_target.population: N: 121 (8 American Indian; 30 Black; 4 Hispanic; 83 White; 1 Other) Age: 70.5 (60-96)

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: Ka = theta1 Vd/F = theta2*exp(eta1) CL/F = theta3*(1+theta4*PHT)*exp(eta2) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1) PHT=comedication with phenytoin (yes=1,no=0)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.population: N: 121 (8 American Indian; 30 Black; 4 Hispanic; 83 White; 1 Other) Age: 70.5 (60-96)
study.objective: “to determine the relevant population pharmacokinetic parameters of carbamazepine in a population of community-dwelling elderly subjects and to evaluate the impact of eighteen commonly available covariates on drug dosing requirements through an effect on clearance.”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, BMI, body surface area, ideal body weight, lean body weight, race (White/Non-white), age, study center, PHT, alcohol use, smoking, ALT, albumin, total protein, blood urea nitrogen, serum creatinine, CLcr (Cockroft-Gault) Included: PHT on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
